== Notes for [[Item:Q105599|Q105599]] ==

[http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Gamliel_V '''Gamliel V גמליאל החמישי''']

'''Gamaliel V''' held the office of Nasi of the ancient Jewish Sanhedrin between 365 and 380/5 CE. He was the son and successor of the Jewish patriarch Hillel II. He was notable for involving himself with perfecting the Jewish calendar in 359. He is apparently referred to in a letter written by Saint Jerome, in which Jerome states that Theodosius I (379-395) had condemned to death one Esychius, a former Roman consul who had stolen documents belonging to Gamaliel, who was furious about it.

*Preceded by Hillel II Nasi
*Sanhedrin president: 365 CE - 380/5 CE
*Succeeded by Judah IV

*<nowiki>------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------</nowiki>

REFERENCE_NUMBERS:
geni:6000000010960575249

[[Category:GEDCOM Notes Pages]][[Category:Pages]]
